steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: f(x) = 28(1.30)^(x/4). We need the percent increase when x increases by 8. An 8-unit increase adds 8/4 = 2 to the exponent, so f is multiplied by (1.30)² = 1.69. The percent increase p satisfies 1 + p/100 = 1.69, so p = 69.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘30’: 30% is the growth factor per 4 units, not per 8. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘48’: This comes from using the wrong exponent change, since 100((1.30)^(3/2) - 1) rounds to 48. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘60’: Doubling 30 ignores compounding (30 + 30 = 60, but the correct answer compounds). ✗
  • Choice D — ‘69’: (1.30)² = 1.69, a 69% increase. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
